text: A patient deliberately seeks out medical investigations for chronic symptoms with no organic cause. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Factitious disorder involves intentional symptom fabrication to assume the sick role [cite: 391-396].

text: A 3-year-old with a painful elbow held in pronation after a traction injury (Nursemaid's elbow). Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Subluxation of the radial head occurs when a child's arm is pulled [cite: 411-416].

text: Trauma patient with pelvic fractures, blood at the meatus, and an elevated prostate. Next step?
rationale: Dx: Urethral injury. [cite_start]Retrograde urethrography must be done before inserting a catheter [cite: 432-437].

text: Relapse rate with drug is 0.3 vs 0.4 with placebo. What is the Number Needed to Treat (NNT)?
Newborn is cyanotic. Oxygenation does not improve with 100% oxygen. Likely cause?
rationale: Dx: Transposition of the Great Vessels is a cyanotic heart defect with minimal response to oxygen [cite: 496-511].

text: Woman with palpitations, low TSH, high FT4, and diffuse uptake on scan. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Graves disease is characterized by hyperthyroidism and diffuse thyroid uptake [cite: 550-556].

text: A 70-year-old smoker has abdominal pain after each meal and weight loss. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Mesenteric ischemia often presents with post-prandial pain and weight loss in smokers [cite: 605-612].

text: Toddler has poor weight gain and loose stools since starting solid foods. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Celiac disease often presents with failure to thrive after gluten is introduced [cite: 636-640].

text: Severe back pain after a fall, exam shows a pulsatile mid-abdominal mass. Best test?
rationale: Dx: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm (AAA). [cite_start]CT or Ultrasound is diagnostic [cite: 676-681].

text: A 6-week-old has Hgb 140 (down from 220 at birth). She is healthy. Likely reason?
rationale: Dx: Physiological anemia of infancy is a normal decline in Hgb levels after birth [cite: 701-706].
